reason: Disabling host-based authentication is a standard SSH hardening measure that aligns with STIG recommendations. It does not affect primary SSH access methods (e.g., password or key-based) and is unlikely to cause lockout or system breakage, as the default configuration already disables this feature.
estimated_impact: authentication/authorization affected; filesystems affected; Potential misconfiguration if remediation script is applied incorrectly; Could affect legacy applications relying on host-based authentication
